title: Crepidium angustifoveum
intro: Crepidium angustifoveum, also known as the 'Purple Pansy Orchid', is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepidium angustifoveum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Crepidium angustifoveum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Crepidium angustifoveum is a species of orchid. It is classified within the family Orchidaceae. The genus Crepidium is part of the subtribe Cranichidinae and tribe Cranichideae. Crepidium angustifoveum is native to Central and South America. It is a terrestrial orchid, typically growing in shaded, moist environments.
